commit.message:
Fix/async (#685)

* feat: native async Redis for conversation streaming (AsyncRedisStreamManager)

- Add AsyncRedisStreamManager (redis.asyncio) for FastAPI routes
- Add AsyncSessionService with scan_iter instead of keys()
- conversation_routing: async_ensure_unique_run_id, async start_celery_task_and_stream/wait
- Routers use async Redis/session deps; stop_generation uses async when injected
- Lifespan: create AsyncRedisStreamManager on startup, aclose on shutdown
- Sync RedisStreamManager.wait_for_task_start: require_running=True (correctness)
- ConversationController/Service accept optional async_redis_manager, async_session_service

Made-with: Cursor

* added reddis retry mechanism

* Fail-fast on async Redis init; select active session by stream recency

Made-with: Cursor

* Active session: fallback to next key if chosen key no longer exists

Made-with: Cursor

* Session service: narrow Redis/OSError handling, re-raise infra errors, use logger.exception

Made-with: Cursor

* Step 2a: migrate UsageService to AsyncSession for usage checks

- usage_service: get_usage_data(session, ...) and check_usage_limit(user_id, session) with async select/execute
- usage_controller: get_user_usage accepts session, passes to get_usage_data
- usage_router: inject get_async_db, pass async_db to get_user_usage
- conversations_router + api/router: pass async_db to check_usage_limit, remove dead if-not-checked blocks
- scripts/benchmark_usage_check.py: benchmark POST /conversations for before/after comparison

Made-with: Cursor

* Benchmark: exclude failed requests from latency percentiles

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: AsyncChatHistoryService (Step 2b) and GitHub API timeouts

- Add AsyncChatHistoryService with async get_session_history, flush_message_buffer, save_partial_ai_message
- ConversationService.create() accepts optional async_db; when set, uses AsyncChatHistoryService on FastAPI path
- ConversationController passes async_db to create() for async history
- Celery path unchanged (no async_db) and continues using sync ChatHistoryService
- GitHub service: add connect/read timeouts and HTTPException for timeout/request errors

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: add __future__ annotations to fix undefined type names at import

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: AsyncShareChatService and AsyncUserService (Step 2c)

- Add AsyncShareChatService in access_service.py (share_chat, get_shared_emails, remove_access) using AsyncSession
- conversations_router: share/access endpoints use get_async_db and AsyncShareChatService
- Add AsyncUserService in user_service.py (get_user_by_uid, get_user_id_by_email, get_user_by_email, get_user_ids_by_emails, create_user, update_last_login)
- api/router: get_api_key_user uses AsyncUserService(async_db) for admin-secret user lookup
- Add scripts/benchmark_share_access.py for POST /share, GET /shared-emails, DELETE /access

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: 2d auth AsyncUserService + 2e GithubService async DB (get_combined_user_repos)

2d:
- auth_router: inject get_async_db, use AsyncUserService(async_db) in signup, sso_login, get_my_account
- await get_user_by_uid, get_user_by_email, update_last_login

2e:
- github_service: get_combined_user_repos and get_repos_for_user accept optional async_session; use select() when provided
- code_provider_controller.get_user_repos and github_router pass async_db to get_combined_user_repos

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: use async_get_github_oauth_token in get_repos_for_user when async_session provided

Made-with: Cursor

* Step 3 + 4: Async Redis (tunnel, GitHub/branch cache) and async HTTP clients

Step 3 - Async Redis for non-streaming:
- tunnel_service: async Redis client, get/set_workspace_tunnel_record_async,
  list_user_tunnels_async (scan_iter); sync list_user_tunnels uses scan_iter;
  workspace record TTL
- tunnel_router: use get_workspace_tunnel_record_async
- github_service: lazy async Redis cache for get_project_structure_async
- branch_cache: get_branches_async; controller get_branch_list uses it
- benchmark_tunnel_github.py, docs/async-migration-plan.md

Step 4 - Async HTTP clients:
- auth_service: login_async with httpx.AsyncClient; sync login with httpx.Client
- auth_router: login calls login_async; send_slack_message uses httpx.AsyncClient
- parse_webhook_helper: send_slack_notification uses httpx.AsyncClient
- linear_client: execute_query_async, get_issue_async, update_issue_async,
  comment_create_async (timeout 10s/30s); Linear tools use async methods
- email_helper: resend.Emails.send via asyncio.to_thread
- posthog_helper: send_event uses run_in_executor when loop running
- auth_service_test: patch httpx.Client for login tests

Made-with: Cursor

* Fix edge cases and linter: httpx.Timeout, imports, logging, guards

- auth_service: use httpx.Timeout(connect=, read=) to fix TypeError
- linear_client: same httpx.Timeout fix
- auth_router: move imports to top (E402), consolidate AuthProviderCreate
- github_service: remove f-string prefix where no placeholders (F541)
- branch_cache: guard redis.from_url when redis_url is None
- parse_webhook_helper: use logger instead of print
- email_helper: set resend.api_key only when api_key is set

Made-with: Cursor

* Auth status preservation, Redis cleanup, cache guards

- auth_service: catch HTTPStatusError/HTTPError, raise HTTPException with status
- branch_cache: add aclose() for async and sync Redis clients
- github_service: close_github_async_redis_cache() and call from app shutdown
- github_service: guard project_structure Redis read/write with try/except

Made-with: Cursor

* Auth: wrap post in try, safe logging; GitHub: lock async Redis init

- auth_service: wrap client.post in try so transport errors raise HTTPException
- auth_service: log only status_code for HTTPStatusError, generic msg for HTTPError
- github_service: add asyncio.Lock for _get_async_redis_cache to fix race

Made-with: Cursor

* Offload sync Redis fallback in get_project_structure_async to thread pool

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: GitPython/libgit2 SIGSEGV on macOS (lazy imports + Celery solo pool)

- Lazy-import GitPython in parsing_helper, parsing_service, github_service,
  local_repo_service, local_provider, change_detection_tool to avoid loading
  git at import time (fork-unsafe in gunicorn/Celery workers on macOS).
- parsing_helper: _fetch_github_branch_head_sha_http for commit-status check
  (HTTP-only, no GitPython) so parsing-status endpoint is safe in forked workers.
- Celery: on Darwin use solo pool and set multiprocessing start method to
  spawn to avoid SIGSEGV in forked workers; prefork unchanged on Linux.
- git_safe: SIGSEGV handler uses async-signal-safe os.write/os._exit only.
- parsing_controller: remove debug instrumentation.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: remove dangling _history_add_message_chunk from merge (syntax error)

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: async Redis in stop_generation, async buffer tool_calls/thinking, auth/timeout/posthog/routing/redis fixes

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: git ImportError guard, typing Any, async chunk dispatcher, email timeout helper, run_id stream check

Made-with: Cursor

* chore: refactor code changes manager

* fix: wait_for_task_start require_running=False and status follow-up to avoid 30s block

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: stop functionality

* feat: code changes manager refactor

* chore: checkpoint before stash merge - async migration + multi-agent fixes, remove debug log blocks

Made-with: Cursor

* merge: stash@{0} into fix/async - async management improvements

- celery_app: OTel patch with CELERY_WORKER=1, pydantic_ai instrumentation enabled
- agent_tasks: _clear_pydantic_ai_http_client_cache, sync user resolution before run_async
- base_task: run_async docstring (shutdown_asyncgens context)
- stream_processor: ThinkingPart/ThinkingPartDelta, idle-timeout, ToolCallPart streaming
- execution_flows: CELERY_WORKER MCP skip, remove debug blocks
- history_processor, message_history_utils: major refactor
- redis_streaming, chat_agent, message_compressor, message_helpers, pydantic_agent,
  chat_history_service, parsing_tasks, code_changes_manager, tunnel_utils: minor fixes

Made-with: Cursor

* feat: fix async calls

---------

Co-authored-by: Yash Krishan <yashkmkrishan@gmail.com>

Improve codegen agent to write changes to worktree, fixed pr creation tool not getting necessary context to create a PR (#650)

Update error handling in stream processor to properly manage GenerationCancelled exceptions. Improve worktree write failure handling in CodeChangesManager to return detailed error messages. Clean up imports in create_pr_workflow_tool.py.

commit.message:
Fix api url creation logic by passing necessary prefix based on the login type (#622)

* Refactor RepoManager initialization and enhance authentication logging

- Updated the RepoManager initialization to always enable the repo manager, removing the conditional check for environment variables.
- Improved logging for authentication processes, detailing the type of GitHub token being used and its source (user-provided or environment).
- Enhanced the construction of authenticated URLs to include token type and user context for better traceability.
- Refactored error handling and logging in the repo manager to provide clearer insights during cloning operations.

* Enhance authentication flow in SyncHelper

- Implemented a prioritized authentication chain for GitHub tokens, starting with GitHub App tokens, followed by User OAuth tokens, and finally environment tokens.
- Improved logging to provide detailed insights into the authentication process, including success and failure messages for each token type.
- Refactored error handling to ensure clear communication of issues during authentication attempts.

Restore source /app/.env in supervisord configs to fix CELERY_QUEUE_NAME loading.

on _process_repository/_agent_tasks while mom-api sent to
potpieProdMicroSVC_process_repository/potpieProdMicroSVC_agent_tasks.

Also reverts Jenkinsfile changes (branch handling, rollout undo quoting).

commit.message:
fix: sync/async and Celery parsing fixes for production (#606)

* fix: sync/async and Celery parsing fixes for production

- ProjectService: offload all sync DB calls to asyncio.to_thread with dedicated SessionLocal()
- ParseHelper.check_commit_status: run GitHub get_repo/get_branch in thread pool
- CodeProviderController: offload get_branch_list, check_public_repo, get_user_repos to thread pool
- GithubService.get_combined_user_repos: run DB query in thread pool
- ParsingController.fetch_parsing_status: run status query in thread pool
- Celery parsing_tasks: use ParsingService(..., raise_library_exceptions=True)
- ParsingService: raise ParsingServiceError when extracted_dir is None and raise_library_exceptions=True

Fixes event-loop blocking under load (parse requests reaching Celery) and Celery UnpickleableExceptionWrapper.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: use getattr for project.directory (column dropped in migration)

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* revert: session offloading for ProjectService, github_service, parsing_controller

Use request-scoped db/session again instead of asyncio.to_thread + SessionLocal()
to avoid session lifecycle and connection pool concerns. Keep GitHub API offloading
and Celery raise_library_exceptions unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Feat/vscode extension tunnel (#600)

* feat: add tunnel service for VS Code extension local execution

- Add TunnelService to manage tunnel connections (Redis-backed)
- Add tunnel registration/unregistration endpoints
- Implement tool routing to LocalServer for code agents
- Add code-changes sync endpoint for LocalServer to push changes
- Initialize agent_id and user_id context for tunnel routing
- Support both conversation-specific and user-level tunnel lookup

* Add modular local search tools with tunnel routing

- Created local_search_tools module with 8 search tools (symbols, workspace symbols, references, definitions, files, text, code structure, bash)
- Modularized search tools for maintainability
- Added tunnel routing utilities for LocalServer integration
- Integrated search tools with code_changes_manager
- Added bash/grep support with cloud fallback
- Updated uv.lock with dependencies

* feat: implement two-way fallback for fetch_file tool

- Add local-first routing with GitHub fallback for fetch_file
- If GitHub returns 404, retry LocalServer to handle unpushed files
- Improve error messages with clear guidance on what was checked
- Handle common scenario where files exist locally but not on GitHub
- Add search_semantic_tool and knowledge_graph router for future semantic search integration

* feat: Add named Cloudflare tunnel support

- Add CloudflareTunnelService for provisioning named tunnels via Cloudflare API
- Add /api/v1/tunnels/provision endpoint for extensions to request tunnel tokens
- Named tunnels provide persistent URLs (don't change on restart), 99%+ uptime
- Gracefully falls back to quick tunnels if Cloudflare not configured (503)

Required env vars for named tunnels:
- CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
- CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN

If not configured, extension will automatically use quick tunnels instead.

* fix(tunnel): hybrid named/quick routing + local hairpin bypass

* feat: local mode

* feat: execute terminal command

* fix: prevent 414 URI Too Long error when fetching GitHub repositories

- Use proper URL parsing instead of string concatenation
- Handle cases where repos_url already has query parameters
- Add error handling and logging for long URLs
- Fixes 414 errors in production when fetching repositories from GitHub App installations

* feat: agent updates

* fix: prevent 414 error by not using encrypted tokens as fallback

- Add validation to check if failed-decryption token is actually plaintext
- Only use tokens that look like valid GitHub tokens (< 100 chars, starts with gh*)
- Don't use long encrypted tokens that failed decryption - fall back to system tokens instead
- Add detailed logging to identify encrypted vs plaintext tokens
- Prevents 414 URI Too Long errors caused by passing encrypted tokens to PyGithub

* fix: resolve merge conflict in tunnel_utils.py

- Keep upstream version using get_tunnel_url() method
- Resolve conflict from stashed changes

* commented env setup for deployment testing

* feat: chat before inference

* fix: add tunnel_url form parameter to post_message endpoint

- Extract tunnel_url from form data in conversations router
- Pass tunnel_url to MessageRequest and Celery task
- This enables the VS Code extension's tunnel URL to be used for
  routing search/file operations to LocalServer

The extension was sending tunnel_url but backend wasn't reading it,
causing 'Search requires LocalServer connection' errors.

* fix: make .env file sourcing more robust in supervisord configs

- Use 'set -a; source /app/.env 2>/dev/null || true; set +a' pattern
- This prevents startup failures when .env file has syntax issues
- Suppresses errors and continues execution even if some lines fail
- Fixes 'command not found' errors when sourcing .env files with
  special characters or syntax issues (e.g., META-LLAMA_API_KEY)

* fix: reduce gunicorn workers to 2 to prevent OOMKilled

* fix: reduce gunicorn workers to 1 and remove source .env to prevent OOMKilled and bash errors

- Changed --workers from $(nproc) to 1 in all supervisord configs (mom-api, convo-server, celery)
- Removed source /app/.env from all supervisord configs (prevents bash errors with hyphenated keys like META-LLAMA_API_KEY)
- Added 414 URI Too Long error handling in GitHub service to skip installations with URLs > 2000 chars
- All configs now rely on load_dotenv() in Python code for environment variables

* Fix 414 URI Too Long errors in GitHub API requests

- Add pre-check for original repos_url length before processing
- Fix parsed_url undefined error in pagination when URL parsing fails
- Handle duplicate per_page parameters in URL construction
- Fix invalid query string after removing per_page (?& case)
- Add comprehensive 414 error handling at all levels (installations, first page, pagination)
- Add URL length checks for all pagination URLs
- Improve Link header parsing with error handling for edge cases
- Skip installations with URLs that are too long instead of failing
- Better logging for debugging production issues

* fix: Replace PyGithub get_orgs() with direct API call to prevent 414 errors

- Root cause: PyGithub's get_orgs() method may construct URLs with query parameters that exceed length limits
- Production proxy/load balancer may further modify URLs, pushing them over the limit
- Solution: Use direct GitHub API call with simple URL construction
- Added detailed logging to help debug URL lengths
- This gives us full control over the URL and prevents 414 errors at the source

* feat: code gen changes

* fix: code changes manager

* feat: code apply changes

* Fix Alembic migration dependency chain for 20251209_add_inferring_status

- Fix 20251209_add_inferring_status to depend on 20251202164905_07bea433f543 instead of 20251217190000
- Fix 20251217190000_encrypt_user_auth_provider_tokens to depend on 20251209_add_inferring_status
- Resolves 'Can't locate revision identified by 20251209_add_inferring_status' error in Kubernetes deployment

* feat: history management changes

* Media module updates (controller, router, service)

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* feat: code gen changes

* feat: sub agent tool call streaming

* fix: jenkins file

* fix: build errors

* refactor: update conversation API endpoint paths to remove trailing slashes

* feat: local mode

* fix: tunnel registration

* feat: add RepositoriesResource and runtime.repositories; simplify projects.list to return IDs

- Add potpie.resources.repositories.RepositoriesResource for worktrees/codegen
- Expose runtime.repositories (lazy-initialized)
- projects.list() now returns list of project ID strings

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix: tunneling issues

* fix: tunnel and auth issues

* feat: workspace level tunnels

* feat: websocket connection

* feat: websocket changes

* feat: code gen local prompt

* Repo parsing fix (parsing tested on local repos (#605)

* Fix GitHub App 401: normalize private key newlines and improve error messages

commit.message:
feat: Add work email validation for SSO authentication (#551)

* feat: Add work email validation for SSO authentication

- Block new users with generic email providers (Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook, etc.)
- Block all new GitHub signups (existing users can still sign in)
- Allow legacy users with generic emails to continue using the service
- Add server-side validation in /sso/login and /signup endpoints
- Add email domain validation utilities (extract_domain, is_generic_email)
- Return 403 Forbidden with clear error messages for blocked attempts

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

* fix: Use publicsuffix2 for proper multi-part TLD handling

- Replace simple domain extraction with publicsuffix2.get_sld()
- Properly handles multi-part TLDs like .co.uk, .com.au
- Fixes issue where user@gmail.co.uk was incorrectly extracting to 'co.uk'
- Add fallback logic if library is not available
- Add additional Yahoo country-specific domains to blocked list

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

* refactor: Improve extract_domain with proper email validation and module-level import

- Move publicsuffix2 import to module level with proper error handling
- Add comprehensive email validation and normalization (strip, lower)
- Improve error handling with clear fallback logic
- Add HAS_PUBLICSUFFIX2 flag to check library availability
- Ensure proper domain validation before processing
- Better documentation explaining the normalization process

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

* fix: Remove redundant validation check that creates orphaned users

- Remove secondary validation check after authenticate_or_create
- First validation (lines 645-657) already blocks new users with generic emails
- If user reaches authenticate_or_create with generic email, they must be existing (legacy)
- Existing users return 'success' status, not 'new_user', so second check never triggers
- Removing this prevents potential orphaned user records in edge cases
- Simplifies code flow and removes unnecessary complexity

* fix: Update publicsuffix2 to valid PyPI version

- Replace invalid version 3.0.1 with valid version 2.20191221
- Version 2.20191221 is the latest available on PyPI
- Verified availability using pip index versions

* refactor: Move domain extraction to email_helper and use tldextract

- Replace publicsuffix2 with tldextract (more actively maintained, v5.3.0)
- Move domain extraction logic to app/modules/utils/email_helper.py
- Centralize email-related utilities in email_helper module
- Update auth_router to use extract_registrable_domain from email_helper
- Remove inline import logic from auth_router (cleaner separation of concerns)
- Update requirements.txt to use tldextract==5.3.0 instead of publicsuffix2

* refactor: Remove duplicate email validation code, use email_helper

- Remove local BLOCKED_DOMAINS, extract_domain, and is_generic_email definitions
- Import is_personal_email_domain from app.modules.utils.email_helper
- Replace is_generic_email(email) with is_personal_email_domain(email)
- Eliminate duplicate domain lists - single source of truth in email_helper
- Cleaner code with centralized email validation utilities

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

---------

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Simplify SSO/GitHub linking and use Firebase UID consistently (#546)

* fix: Simplify SSO/GitHub linking and use Firebase UID consistently

- Simplified signup endpoint from 400+ lines to 250 lines with clear flow separation
- Fixed SSO users being incorrectly deleted as 'orphaned records'
- Updated Google SSO to verify Firebase ID tokens (not Google OAuth tokens)
- All authentication now uses Firebase UID consistently (28-char format)
- Fixed GitHub linking to work correctly when GitHub email differs from SSO email
- Removed email-based user lookups to prevent inconsistencies
- Added proper Firebase verification for all Firebase-based providers
- Cleaned up debug-style log formatting

Key changes:
- auth_router.py: Simplified to 3 clear flows (GitHub linking, GitHub sign-in, email/password)
- unified_auth_service.py: Skip Firebase check for SSO users (they don't have Firebase Auth accounts)
- google_provider.py: Verify Firebase ID tokens first, fallback to Google OAuth
- github_service.py: Use unified auth system for GitHub provider lookup

Fixes:
- GitHub linking now persists correctly across sign-in sessions
- SSO users no longer get deleted and recreated on each login
- Consistent UID format across all authentication methods

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

* fix: Address code review issues - unused variable, double encryption, orphaned user deletion

- Fix unused provider_type variable by using it instead of hardcoding constants
- Fix double encryption issue - pass plaintext token to update_last_login
- Extract orphaned user deletion into _delete_orphaned_user helper function
- Add missing child table deletions (prompts, user_preferences, organization_sso_config)
- Ensure proper deletion order to avoid FK constraint violations
- Improve code maintainability and testability

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

* refactor: Remove unused firebase_user variable and use constant for provider type

- Remove unused firebase_user assignment, just call auth.get_user() for existence check
- Replace hardcoded 'firebase_github' string with PROVIDER_TYPE_FIREBASE_GITHUB constant
- Remove duplicate UserAuthProvider import (already imported at top of file)

---------

commit.message:
SSO implementation (#506)

* feat(auth): Add SSO database schema and models

Phase 1: Foundation & Database

New Models:
- UserAuthProvider: Multiple auth providers per user
- PendingProviderLink: Temporary storage for provider linking
- OrganizationSSOConfig: SSO configuration for domains
- AuthAuditLog: Security audit trail

User Model Updates:
- Add organization & organization_name fields
- Add auth_providers relationship
- Add helper methods (get_primary_provider, has_provider)

Migration:
- Create all new tables with indexes for performance
- Migrate existing users to user_auth_providers
- Auto-extract organization from email domains
- Backward compatible with existing Firebase auth

Verified:
- Migration successful
- 1 existing user migrated to new schema
- All indexes created
- Foreign keys and constraints working

* test: Add comprehensive unit tests and implementation guides

Tests Added:
- 40+ unit tests for UnifiedAuthService
- 20+ tests for auth models
- Integration test placeholders for API endpoints
- pytest configuration

Test Coverage:
✓ authenticate_or_create() - all 3 scenarios
✓ Provider management (add, remove, set primary)
✓ Account linking flow
✓ Pending link confirmation/cancellation
✓ SSO configuration
✓ Audit logging
✓ Model constraints and relationships
✓ Cascade deletes

Implementation Guides:
- Backend guide (300+ lines)
  - Architecture overview
  - Database layer documentation
  - Service layer API reference
  - SSO provider integration
  - Common patterns
  - Troubleshooting

- Frontend guide (700+ lines)
  - Complete component implementation
  - TypeScript types
  - SSO button components
  - Account linking dialog
  - Account settings page
  - Testing checklist
  - Deployment guide

Ready to run: pytest app/modules/auth/tests/

* feat(auth): Add unified auth service and SSO endpoints

Phase 2: Unified Authentication Service

New Service (UnifiedAuthService):
- Multi-provider authentication and management
- Smart account linking with confirmation flow
- Provider preferences (primary/secondary)
- Audit logging for all auth events
- Organization domain detection

API Schemas (auth_schema.py):
- AuthProviderCreate/Response for provider management
- SSOLoginRequest/Response for SSO flows
- LinkProviderRequest/ConfirmLinkingRequest for account linking
- OrganizationSSOConfig schemas
- AccountResponse with complete user info

New Endpoints:
- POST /api/v1/sso/login - SSO authentication
- POST /api/v1/providers/confirm-linking - Confirm provider linking
- DELETE /api/v1/providers/cancel-linking/{token} - Cancel pending link
- GET /api/v1/providers/me - Get user's providers
- POST /api/v1/providers/set-primary - Set primary provider
- DELETE /api/v1/providers/unlink - Unlink provider
- GET /api/v1/account/me - Get complete account info

Key Features:
- Email-based identity to prevent duplication
- Three authentication scenarios handled:
  1. Existing user + provider → Login
  2. Existing user + new provider → Confirm linking
  3. New user → Create account
- 15-minute expiring linking tokens for security
- Cannot unlink last provider (prevents lockout)
- Auto-extract organization from email domain

Backward Compatible:
- Existing /login and /signup endpoints unchanged
- Works alongside Firebase auth
- Migrated users work seamlessly

* feat(auth): Add SSO provider integrations and documentation

Phase 3: SSO Provider Integration

SSO Provider Framework:
- BaseSSOProvider: Abstract interface for all SSO providers
- Standardized SSOUserInfo data class
- Token verification interface
- OAuth authorization URL generation

Google SSO Provider:
- ID token verification using google-auth library
- Support for Google Workspace and personal Gmail
- Hosted domain restriction option
- Extracts: email, name, picture, verified status

Azure AD Provider:
- ID token verification using PyJWT + JWKS
- Support for Azure AD / Microsoft 365 / Entra ID
- Multi-tenant and single-tenant support
- Extracts: email, name, object ID, verified status

UnifiedAuthService Integration:
- Auto-loads SSO providers (Google, Azure)
- verify_sso_token() method for server-side verification
- get_sso_provider() for dynamic provider selection

Comprehensive API Documentation:
- Complete endpoint reference
- Authentication flow diagrams
- Request/response examples
- Error handling guide
- Security best practices
- Frontend integration examples
- Testing instructions
- Migration guide

Ready for Production:
- All linters pass
- No breaking changes
- Backward compatible with Firebase auth
- Environment variable configuration
- Extensible for new providers (Okta, SAML)

Dependencies Used:
- google-auth==2.43.0 (already in requirements.txt)
- pyjwt==2.10.1 (already in requirements.txt)

* Resolve merge conflict and update email helper usage

* Implement singleton pattern for SSO providers

- Create SSOProviderRegistry with thread-safe singleton pattern
- SSO providers are stateless and can be safely shared across requests
- Reduces memory usage and initialization overhead
- Each provider type is instantiated once and reused
- Update UnifiedAuthService to use singleton provider instances
- Add test to verify singleton behavior

* Remove Azure SSO authentication support

- Delete azure_provider.py implementation
- Remove Azure from SSO provider registry
- Update documentation and comments
- Remove Azure from test cases

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

* Implement token encryption for UserAuthProvider

- Add encryption when writing tokens in unified_auth_service.py
- Add decryption when reading tokens in github_service.py
- Add helper methods get_decrypted_access_token() and get_decrypted_refresh_token()
- Update tests to handle encrypted tokens
- Create migration to encrypt existing plaintext tokens
- Update model comments to reflect encryption
- Follows same pattern as Integration model token encryption

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

* refactor: Replace f-strings in logging with % formatting

- Replace all f-string logging calls with % formatting in SSO/auth modules
- Update datetime.utcnow() to datetime.now(timezone.utc) in test fixtures
- Remove duplicate entries from PERSONAL_EMAIL_DOMAINS set
- Improve logging consistency across authentication codebase

* fix: Fix indentation and logging issues

- Fix indentation in unified_auth_service.py _create_pending_link method
- Add logging statement in setup_logging_middleware for observability
- Update potpie-ui submodule with frontend fixes

---------

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: fix issues with celery uv run

Migrate pip to uv (#479)

* migrate all dependencies to use uv

* migrate tooling and startup scripts to use uv; bump Python to 3.11

- Dockerfile: switch base image to python:3.11-slim, install supervisor, copy uv binary, use pyproject.toml + uv.lock and `uv sync` to install deps, set VIRTUAL_ENV/PATH, run NLTK download via `uv run`.
- .github/workflows/pre-commit.yml: install uv in workflow and install pre-commit using `uv tool install` instead of pip.
- start.sh / start.ps1: require `uv`, run `uv sync`, run migrations and services via `uv run` (alembic, uvicorn/gunicorn, celery); remove direct pip/.venv installs.
- start_event_listener.sh / start_event_worker.sh: remove .venv checks and use `uv run` to start Python and Celery.
- stop.sh: comment out `docker compose down` to avoid automatically tearing down services.

* migrate old/depreceated pydantic and langchain to the latest version compatible with python 3.11

* update docs & guides to use uv instead of pip

* migrate prod and staging Dockerfiles to use uv and bump Python to 3.11

- update base images from python:3.10-slim to python:3.11-slim
- replace requirements/pip installs with astral-sh/uv: copy uv, copy pyproject.toml & uv.lock, run `uv sync`
- run nltk downloads via `uv run python ...`
- ensure virtualenv bin on PATH (VIRTUAL_ENV/PATH)
- install supervisor in image and clean apt cache
- update .gitignore to include .ruff_cache and .pytest_cache

* Add persistent named volumes for postgres, neo4j, and redis in docker-compose.yaml

- This makes sure that shutting down the containers keeps the data persistent

* re-enable docker compose down in stop.sh to stop Docker Compose services

* Update pre-commit config: bump hooks, remove black, enable ruff-format, and add Bandit settings

- bump pre-commit-hooks to v5.0.0
- bump ruff-pre-commit to v0.8.2 and add ruff-format (exclude ^app/alembic/)
- remove Black hook
- bump Bandit to 1.7.10 and add args/additional_dependencies/exclude for TOML config
- add [tool.bandit] block to pyproject.toml

* pin uv image to 0.9.6 in Dockerfile for reproducible builds

* Pin astral-sh/uv image to 0.9.6 in prod and stage Dockerfiles for reproducible builds

* Add ruff configuration to exclude specific directories

* Update dependencies after merging with main

* chore: update langchain-core to version 1.1.0 in requirements and uv.lock

commit.message: hotfix: update Structured tools import to follow the current langchain version

Confluence integration (#481)

* Add Jira OAuth integration: OAuth helper, schemas, service logic and API routes

- Add new jira_oauth module implementing JiraOAuth and in-memory token store (3LO flow, token exchange, accessible-resources, revoke)
- Add Jira-specific Pydantic schemas (JiraIntegrationStatus, JiraSaveRequest, JiraSaveResponse)
- Extend IntegrationsService with Jira support:
  - register JiraOAuth instance, get_jira_oauth_instance()
  - check_existing_jira_integration(), save_jira_integration(), _get_jira_context()
  - get_jira_accessible_resources(), get_jira_projects(), get_jira_project_details()
  - get_jira_integration_status(), deactivate_jira_integrations_for_user()
- Update integrations_router: add Jira dependency, endpoints for initiate, callback, save, status, revoke, resources, projects and project details
- Use httpx for Jira API calls, token encryption for stored tokens, and add logging/error handling

This adds end-to-end Jira integration support (authorization, token handling, persistence and API surface).

* Add Jira webhook handling: JWT verification, endpoint, registration & cleanup

- Add verify_jira_webhook_jwt helper and /jira/webhook FastAPI endpoint to validate Jira-signed webhooks (JWT), parse payload, log via IntegrationsService, and publish events to the Celery event bus.
- Attempt to resolve integration_id by matchedWebhookIds + site_id (from JWT, payload or headers), fall back to site lookup or explicit integration_id/header.
- IntegrationsService: register Jira webhook when saving an integration (store webhook metadata), add _cleanup_jira_webhooks called before deletion, and add log_jira_webhook helper.
- JiraOAuth: fix default JIRA_OAUTH_SCOPE value, implement create_webhook and delete_webhook methods to manage Jira Cloud webhooks (HTTP create/delete with payload handling).
- Add PyJWT dependency to requirements for JWT decoding/verification.

* Fix Jira webhook cleanup and deletion: read auth_data/scope_data, always decrypt tokens, use bulk delete API

- integrations_service: use db_integration.auth_data (not metadata) for access_token, derive site_id from scope_data or metadata, add debug logging on missing data, and always run decrypt_token
- jira_oauth: switch to bulk webhook delete endpoint, send webhookIds array in JSON, add Content-Type header, accept 202/204 responses and log successful deletion

* Add Jira site_id lookup and webhook cleanup on deactivation; remove noisy JWT logs

- Add get_jira_integration_by_site_id to query active Jira integrations by cloud/site ID (unique_identifier) with info/warn/error logging.
- Enhance deactivate_jira_integrations_for_user to run _cleanup_jira_webhooks for each integration created by the user before deactivating, logging failures but continuing.
- Remove redundant/verbose logging statements from Jira webhook JWT verification in integrations_router.

* make sure the webhook is not processed if JIRA_CLIENT_SECRET is
not provided; format files

* Add Jira OAuth token refresh: implement refresh_access_token and auto-refresh in _get_jira_context

- Add JiraOAuth.refresh_access_token to call Atlassian token endpoint and return refreshed tokens.
- Extend IntegrationsService._get_jira_context with auto_refresh flag to detect expired tokens, decrypt refresh token, call refresh endpoint, re-encrypt and persist new tokens (including expires_at), and update DB.
- Add logging for refresh successes/failures.

* Add Jira integration tools and client

- Add app/modules/intelligence/tools/jira_tools package:
  - jira_client.py: OAuth2 (3LO) JiraClient using api.atlassian.com (ADF <-> text helpers, issue CRUD, search, comments, transitions, projects, assignment, token expiry check and auto-refresh via IntegrationsService)
  - Structured tool modules: get_jira_issue, search_jira_issues, create_jira_issue, update_jira_issue, add_jira_comment, transition_jira_issue, get_jira_projects (async arun + sync run, Pydantic input schemas, integration checks)
  - __init__.py exporting tools and check_jira_integration_exists
  - README.md documenting usage, auth flow, token refresh, error handling, and dependencies
- Register new Jira tools in ToolService so they're available to agents

This introduces the Jira client and a set of LangChain-compatible tools for agents to interact with Jira on behalf of users.

* Add Jira issue-management tools to system agents

Register Jira-related tools (get_jira_issue, search_jira_issues, create_jira_issue,
update_jira_issue, add_jira_comment, transition_jira_issue, get_jira_projects)
with system agents: general_purpose, code_gen, debug, low_level_design, and qna.
Also expose update_linear_issue in CodeGen, Debug, and QnA agents.

* code lint with black

* Add "Get Jira Project Details" and "Link Jira Issues" tools; extend Jira client and integrate into agents

- Add new tools:
  - tools/jira_tools/get_jira_project_details_tool.py — fetch comprehensive project metadata (issue types, priorities, statuses, link types, labels, lead, etc.)
  - tools/jira_tools/link_jira_issues_tool.py — create links between two Jira issues
  - Export and register both tools in tools/jira_tools/__init__.py and ToolService

- Extend JiraClient:
  - Add get_project_details, get_project_users, and link_issues methods
  - Support include_comments in search_issues and fetch comments per-issue when requested
  - Normalize assignee field to use accountId when creating issues

- Update tools and agents:
  - Wire new tools into ToolService and add to system agents' allowed tool lists (CodeGen, Debug, GeneralPurpose, LowLevelDesign, QnA)
  - Add include_comments option to search_jira_issues_tool and propagate to client
  - Improve Create/Update Jira Issue tool descriptions, guidance to use Get Jira Project Details, and default issue_type behavior

- Jira OAuth:
  - Add manage:jira-configuration to default JIRA_OAUTH_SCOPE

Small documentation and message improvements across Jira tools and clients for clearer guidance and error handling.

* Add "Get Jira Project Users" tool and assignee support for Jira integration

- Add new tool: app/modules/intelligence/tools/jira_tools/get_jira_project_users_tool.py
  - Returns assignable users (account IDs, display names, emails) for a project
  - Exposed via StructuredTool "Get Jira Project Users"
- Export new tool in jira_tools.__init__ and register it in ToolService
- Enable "get_jira_project_users" in system agents' tool lists (CodeGen, Debug, GeneralPurpose, LowLevelDesign, QnA)
- Add assignee support:
  - CreateJiraIssueInput/Tool: add assignee_id, update defaults/docs (issue_type default -> "Task")
  - UpdateJiraIssueInput/Tool: add assignee_id, propagate to update logic and docs
  - JiraClient: accept assignee_id in create_issue and update_issue, include assignee in payload
- Miscellaneous docs/formatting tweaks:
  - Clarify statuses vs. issue types/priorities in GetJiraProjectDetails docs
  - Minor formatting and doc improvements in search, link, and transition tools

* Add Jira tool run/response/call/result messages to tool_helpers; fix None checks and exception handling

* Add Confluence OAuth integration using Atlassian 3LO

- Implemented ConfluenceOAuth class for handling OAuth flows specific to Confluence.
- Added routes for initiating OAuth, handling callbacks, and managing integration status.
- Created schemas for Confluence integration status and save requests/responses.
- Integrated Confluence OAuth into the existing integrations service, including token management and resource fetching.
- Updated integrations router to include Confluence-specific endpoints.
- Refactored JiraOAuth to inherit from a common AtlassianOAuthBase class for shared functionality.

* Add Confluence tools for page management and searching

- Implemented Get Confluence Page Tool to fetch content and metadata of a specific Confluence page by ID.
- Created Get Confluence Space Pages Tool to list all pages within a specific Confluence space.
- Developed Get Confluence Spaces Tool to retrieve available Confluence spaces for the user.
- Added Search Confluence Pages Tool to search for pages using CQL (Confluence Query Language).
- Introduced Update Confluence Page Tool to update existing Confluence pages with new content or titles.
- Integrated all new tools into the ToolService for easy access and functionality.

* Add Confluence integration tools and update tool helpers for Confluence operations

* Enhance Confluence integration: add new OAuth scope and update CQL search tool documentation

* Refactor CQL query string formatting in SearchConfluencePagesInput documentation

* Remove duplocated code

* Refactor Jira and Confluence tools to use async/await for improved performance and responsiveness

* Remove logging of decrypted access token in ConfluenceClient initialization for security reasons

* feat: implement HMAC signing for OAuth state and user association in Confluence integration

* fix: remove duplicate filter for active linear integrations and update metadata retrieval for Confluence integration

* feat: add user authentication checks for Confluence integration status; remove duplicate code created due to merge conflicts

* refactor: remove unused Jira webhook cleanup method from IntegrationsService

* refactor: streamline Confluence and Jira client usage with async context management

* Lint with ruff

* refactor: clean up whitespace in Confluence tools documentation; pre-commit hook

* refactor: simplify Confluence client usage by removing async context manager

commit.message:
Add Jira oAuth Integration with Webhook Support (#474)

* Add Jira OAuth integration: OAuth helper, schemas, service logic and API routes

- Add new jira_oauth module implementing JiraOAuth and in-memory token store (3LO flow, token exchange, accessible-resources, revoke)
- Add Jira-specific Pydantic schemas (JiraIntegrationStatus, JiraSaveRequest, JiraSaveResponse)
- Extend IntegrationsService with Jira support:
  - register JiraOAuth instance, get_jira_oauth_instance()
  - check_existing_jira_integration(), save_jira_integration(), _get_jira_context()
  - get_jira_accessible_resources(), get_jira_projects(), get_jira_project_details()
  - get_jira_integration_status(), deactivate_jira_integrations_for_user()
- Update integrations_router: add Jira dependency, endpoints for initiate, callback, save, status, revoke, resources, projects and project details
- Use httpx for Jira API calls, token encryption for stored tokens, and add logging/error handling

This adds end-to-end Jira integration support (authorization, token handling, persistence and API surface).

* Add Jira webhook handling: JWT verification, endpoint, registration & cleanup

- Add verify_jira_webhook_jwt helper and /jira/webhook FastAPI endpoint to validate Jira-signed webhooks (JWT), parse payload, log via IntegrationsService, and publish events to the Celery event bus.
- Attempt to resolve integration_id by matchedWebhookIds + site_id (from JWT, payload or headers), fall back to site lookup or explicit integration_id/header.
- IntegrationsService: register Jira webhook when saving an integration (store webhook metadata), add _cleanup_jira_webhooks called before deletion, and add log_jira_webhook helper.
- JiraOAuth: fix default JIRA_OAUTH_SCOPE value, implement create_webhook and delete_webhook methods to manage Jira Cloud webhooks (HTTP create/delete with payload handling).
- Add PyJWT dependency to requirements for JWT decoding/verification.

* Fix Jira webhook cleanup and deletion: read auth_data/scope_data, always decrypt tokens, use bulk delete API

- integrations_service: use db_integration.auth_data (not metadata) for access_token, derive site_id from scope_data or metadata, add debug logging on missing data, and always run decrypt_token
- jira_oauth: switch to bulk webhook delete endpoint, send webhookIds array in JSON, add Content-Type header, accept 202/204 responses and log successful deletion

* Add Jira site_id lookup and webhook cleanup on deactivation; remove noisy JWT logs

- Add get_jira_integration_by_site_id to query active Jira integrations by cloud/site ID (unique_identifier) with info/warn/error logging.
- Enhance deactivate_jira_integrations_for_user to run _cleanup_jira_webhooks for each integration created by the user before deactivating, logging failures but continuing.
- Remove redundant/verbose logging statements from Jira webhook JWT verification in integrations_router.

* make sure the webhook is not processed if JIRA_CLIENT_SECRET is
not provided; format files

* Add Jira OAuth token refresh: implement refresh_access_token and auto-refresh in _get_jira_context

- Add JiraOAuth.refresh_access_token to call Atlassian token endpoint and return refreshed tokens.
- Extend IntegrationsService._get_jira_context with auto_refresh flag to detect expired tokens, decrypt refresh token, call refresh endpoint, re-encrypt and persist new tokens (including expires_at), and update DB.
- Add logging for refresh successes/failures.

* Add Jira integration tools and client

- Add app/modules/intelligence/tools/jira_tools package:

commit.message:
Fix dev mode to support both GitHub and local repos (#477)

* Fix dev mode to support both GitHub and local repos

Previously in development mode, the system could not handle GitHub
repositories when GitHub credentials were configured. Two issues:

1. parsing_controller.py always converted repo_name to repo_path in dev mode
2. CodeProviderService always used LocalRepoService in dev mode

Changes:
- parsing_controller.py: Only clear repo_name if both repo_path and repo_name
  are provided (prioritize local). Otherwise keep whichever is provided.
- config_provider.py: Added is_github_configured() helper method
- code_provider_service.py: Changed from choosing one service globally to
  dynamically routing requests:
  * Stores both GithubService and LocalRepoService instances
  * get_repo(): Routes based on whether repo_name is a local path
  * get_project_structure_async(): Routes based on project's repo_path field
  * get_file_content(): Routes based on project's repo_path field

This allows development mode to work with both local repositories and
GitHub repositories when credentials are configured.

Fixes issue where dhirenmathur/test-mongo was treated as local path.

* Update cp client

* chore: Auto-fix pre-commit issues

releases.name: v1.0.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed

This is a major release in the direction of spec-driven development of complex features In large codebases. It includes a lot of context engineering development along with the infrastructure to research, plan, and implement complex changes. The coding agent included in this release was able to achieve 63% on SWE Bench Lite, placing it ahead of the leaderboard. This also includes some changes required for the VSCode extension to be able to leverage Potpie's knowledge graph infrastructure and agents while interacting with the local developer terminal. 

## Highlights

- Introduced **Subagents** through a new multi-agent supervisor/delegation architecture for focused, isolated task execution.
- Introduced **To-do list tooling** for agents to track progress, manage tasks, and coordinate multi-step work.
- Improved **coding agents** with stronger structured workflows (research / plan / implement style execution), better orchestration, and follow-up agent improvements.
- Added and stabilized **bash tool support via Repo Manager**, enabling more reliable local repository/worktree operations for agent workflows.

## Agent & Coding Experience

- Major agent revamp and follow-up improvements to orchestration, streaming, tool usage, and coding task execution.
- Added PR-oriented tools to coding workflows (commit/push/PR/comment support) for more end-to-end automation.
- Improved multi-agent tracing/observability for debugging delegated execution.
- Added VS Code extension tunnel/local workspace support for stronger local execution flows.

## Repo Manager, Parsing, and Local Execution

- Added Repo Manager integration with parsing/worktree registration for consistent repository management.
- Improved bash and local code tool reliability with Repo Manager-backed worktrees.
- Improved worktree creation/recreation logic, including legacy/non-bare repo handling and worktree-only parsing paths.
- Added branch fetch/cache/search improvements and repo/branch UX refinements.
- Improved parsing reliability with inference caching and multiple Celery/parsing production fixes.

## Integrations & Platform Features

- Expanded integrations including Slack, Linear, Confluence, and Jira OAuth + webhooks.
- Expanded extensible code provider support (including Gitbucket).
- Added image support in chat and multimodal feature-flag support.
- Added session management endpoints with streaming support and conversation API improvements.

## Auth & Account Management

- Introduced gmail SSO support.
- Added work email validation for SSO authentication.

## Reliability, Security, and Ops

- Shipped broad dependency and security hardening updates (including CVE-related fixes).
- Migrated from `pip` to `uv` and cleaned up dependencies.
- Improved logging/observability (including Logfire and broader error-handling/logging updates).

## Docs & DX

- README redesign and documentation updates.
- Added new issue templates and performed repository cleanup/reorganization.
- Included potpie-ui updates and UI refresh improvements.

## New Contributors
* @emmanuel-ferdman made their first contribution in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/410
* @Zingzy made their first contribution in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/473
* @yashkrishan made their first contribution in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/506
* @kshitij2040 made their first contribution in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/573

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/compare/v0.1.7...v1.0.0

## What's Changed
* Reduce verbose logging in custom agents service by @sankalpchandra in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/383
* Added the Slack Integration in README file by @ayush2390 in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/387
* Add stop scripts for server by @prajaktad18 in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/390
* fix: logger propogation by @nndn in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/391
* fix: Improved Error handling for api_key_service by @harshit078 in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/385
* Setting usage limits on conversation and API routers by @kinshuksinghbist in https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie/pull/343

readme_html:
Potpie

Potpie turns your entire codebase into a knowledge graph - a structural index of every file, class, and function, capturing all their relationships and what each part of the code does in context of everything else. AI agents built on this graph can reason about your code with the precision of someone who wrote it - from debugging to feature development.

Quick Start

Prerequisites

  • Docker installed and running
  • Git installed
  • Python 3.11+ with uv

Installation

  1. Clone the repository

    git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/potpie-ai/potpie.git
cd potpie

  2. Configure your environment

    cp .env.template .env

    Edit .env with the following required values:

    # App & Environment
isDevelopmentMode=enabled
ENV=development
defaultUsername=defaultuser

# AI / LLM Configuration
LLM_PROVIDER=openai                    # openai | ollama | anthropic | openrouter
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-proj-your-key
CHAT_MODEL=gpt-4o
INFERENCE_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini

# Database
POSTGRES_SERVER=postgresql://postgres:mysecretpassword@localhost:5432/momentum
NEO4J_URI=bolt://127.0.0.1:7687
NEO4J_USERNAME=neo4j
NEO4J_PASSWORD=mysecretpassword

# Redis & Background Jobs
REDISHOST=127.0.0.1
REDISPORT=6379
BROKER_URL=redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0
CELERY_QUEUE_NAME=dev

# Project & Repo Management
PROJECT_PATH=projects

    CHAT_MODEL and INFERENCE_MODEL are used for agent reasoning and knowledge graph generation respectively. Model names follow the provider/model_name format as expected by LiteLLM.

    💡 Using Ollama instead? Set LLM_PROVIDER=ollama and use CHAT_MODEL=ollama_chat/qwen2.5-coder:7b and INFERENCE_MODEL=ollama_chat/qwen2.5-coder:7b.

    See .env.template for the full list of optional configuration (logging, feature flags, object storage, email, analytics, etc.).

  3. Install dependencies

    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
uv sync

  4. Start all services

    chmod +x scripts/start.sh
./scripts/start.sh

    This will start Docker services, apply migrations, start the FastAPI app, and start the Celery worker.

  5. Health Check

    curl -X GET 'http://localhost:8001/health'

  6. Check parsing status

    curl -X GET 'http://localhost:8001/api/v1/parsing-status/your-project-id'

To stop all services:

./scripts/stop.sh

Now set up Potpie Frontend

cd potpie-ui

cp .env.template .env

pnpm build && pnpm start

How it works?

Potpie parses your repository into a knowledge graph stored in Neo4j - capturing every file, function, class, and the relationships between them. Agents read directly from this graph to answer questions and complete tasks grounded in your actual code.

Architecture

  • FastAPI serves as the API layer - all requests enter through localhost:8001 with CORS, Logfire tracing, and optional Sentry error tracking.
  • Firebase Auth handles production authentication. In development mode a dummy user is created locally - no Firebase needed.
  • Celery Worker with Redis as the broker handles async repo parsing - cloning, AST extraction, and knowledge graph construction run entirely in the background.
  • Conversation Service manages chat sessions and agent memory across multi-turn interactions.
  • Agent Router dispatches prompts to the correct pre-built or custom agent based on intent.
  • Tool Service exposes callable functions to agents - code search, file fetch, knowledge graph queries, web tools, and more.
  • Neo4j Knowledge Graph stores your codebase as a property graph - functions, classes, files, imports, and call relationships - the backbone of every agent's context.
  • PostgreSQL stores users, projects, conversations, and message history.

GitHub Authentication

Method Configuration Best For
GitHub App GITHUB_APP_ID, GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY Production
PAT Pool GH_TOKEN_LIST=ghp_token1,ghp_token2 Development / Higher rate limits
Unauthenticated No configuration required Public repositories only (60 req/hr)

Set GITHUB_AUTH_MODE to app, pat, or none to select the method.

Self-Hosted Git Providers

For self-hosted Git servers (e.g., GitBucket, GitLab, etc.), configure:

  uv sync

This will create a .venv directory and install all dependencies from pyproject.toml

GitHub Authentication Setup

Potpie supports multiple authentication methods for accessing GitHub repositories:

For GitHub.com Repositories:

Option 1: GitHub App (Recommended for Production)

  • Create a GitHub App in your organization
  • Set environment variables: 
    GITHUB_APP_ID=your-app-id
GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY=your-private-key

Option 2: Personal Access Token (PAT) Pool

  • Create one or more GitHub PATs with repo scope
  • Set environment variable (comma-separated for multiple tokens): 
    GH_TOKEN_LIST=ghp_token1,ghp_token2,ghp_token3
  • Potpie will randomly select from the pool for load balancing
  • Rate Limit: 5,000 requests/hour per token (authenticated)

Option 3: Unauthenticated Access (Public Repos Only)

  • No configuration needed
  • Automatically used as fallback for public repositories
  • Rate Limit: 60 requests/hour per IP (very limited)
For Self-Hosted Git Servers (GitBucket, GitLab, etc.):
  Set the following environment variables:
  

  CODE_PROVIDER=github   # Options: github, gitbucket
  CODE_PROVIDER_BASE_URL=http://your-git-server.com/api/v3
  CODE_PROVIDER_TOKEN=your-token

Important: GH_TOKEN_LIST tokens are always used for GitHub.com, regardless of CODE_PROVIDER_BASE_URL.

  1. Start Potpie

    To start all Potpie services:

    chmod +x scripts/start.sh
./scripts/start.sh

    This will:

    • Start required Docker services
    • Wait for PostgreSQL to be ready
    • Apply database migrations
    • Start the FastAPI application
    • Start the Celery worker

    Optional: Logfire Tracing Setup

    To monitor LLM traces and agent operations with Pydantic Logfire:

    1. Get a Logfire token from https://logfire.pydantic.dev
    2. Add it to your .env file:
    LOGFIRE_TOKEN=your_token_here
    1. Tracing is automatically initialized when Potpie starts. View traces at https://logfire.pydantic.dev

    Note: Set LOGFIRE_SEND_TO_CLOUD=false in your .env to disable sending traces to Logfire cloud.

  2. Stop Potpie

    To stop all Potpie services:

    ./scripts/stop.sh

    Windows

    ./stop.ps1

    This will gracefully stop:

    • The FastAPI application
    • The Celery worker
    • All Docker Compose services

Potpie's Prebuilt Agents

Potpie offers a suite of specialized codebase agents for automating and optimizing key aspects of software development:

Custom Agents

With Custom Agents, you can design personalized tools that handle repeatable tasks with precision. Define:

  • System Instructions - The agent's task, goal, and expected output
  • Tasks - Individual steps for job completion
  • Tools - Functions for querying the knowledge graph or retrieving code
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8001/api/v1/custom-agents/agents/auto" \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"prompt": "An agent that takes stacktrace as input and gives root cause analysis and proposed solution as output"}'

Read more in our documentation.
